methods.description: Analysis of radial hyphal growth, conidia production, and conidia on carcass data using one-way ANOVA and Tukey's HSD test to compare means at a 5% level of significance. Analyses were performed using DPS and SPSS software.

steps.description: Radial hyphal growth... were analyzed by one-way ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA on radial hyphal growth data.

steps.description: ...conidia production of M. flavoviride... were analyzed by one-way ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA on conidia production data.

steps.description: ...as well as the data of number of conidia on the carcass, were analyzed by one-way ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA on conidia on carcass data.
outputs: ANOVA results for conidia on carcass

steps.description: Means were compared using Tukey's HSD test at a 5% level of significance.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey's HSD post-hoc test for mean comparison.
outputs: Results of Tukey's HSD test (mean comparisons)

steps.description: All data were analyzed using the Data Processing System (DPS) software, version 14.0 [56]...
steps.searchable_summary: Execute statistical analysis using DPS software.

steps.description: ...as well as the SPSS statistical software, version 20.0.
steps.searchable_summary: Execute statistical analysis using SPSS software.

methods.description: Analysis of bioassay data (insect mortality over time at different fungal conidial suspension concentrations) using the TCM model to determine interactions between M. flavoviride and insects. This involves correcting observed mortality with background mortality from controls and fitting the corrected data to the conditional TCM model.

steps.description: Briefly, the adults and second instar larvae of WFT mortality (q ij ), called the conditional mortality probability, caused by a given conidial suspension concentration of M. flavoviride (d i ) at specific time intervals [t j−1 , t j ] (i.e., day 1, day 2, . . . , day 10 after immersion treatment) Insects 2020, 11, 93 6 of 20 were...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect insect mortality data over time at different concentrations.
inputs: Bioassay experiments (insect mortality counts, time points, concentrations)

steps.description: ...corrected with background mortalities (mortality was only treated with sterilized 0.05% TWEEN ® 80) observed from the controls of each assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect background mortality data from control groups.
inputs: Control group experiments (mortality counts over time)

steps.description: ...corrected with background mortalities (mortality was only treated with sterilized 0.05% TWEEN ® 80) observed from the controls of each assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Correct observed mortality data using background mortality.

steps.description: This was then fitted to the conditional TCM model
steps.searchable_summary: Fit corrected mortality data to the conditional Time-Concentration-Mortality (TCM) model.

steps.description: As the Time-Concentration-Mortality (TCM) model can combine time and dose effects into a single model, the interactions of time and dose effects can be examined to reflect the integrity and objectivity of bioassay data. Therefore, the data from the different bioassays (i.e., 1-4) were analyzed using the TCM model to determine the interactions between M. flavoviride and the adults and second instar larvae of WFT [57][58][59].
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze time and dose interactions using the fitted TCM model.
outputs: Analysis of time and dose interactions
